title: akoúō Standard Listening Output
description: Common output structure for every AKOÚŌ listening mode. In v0.8 the optional listening_context block makes position, apertures, scale, participants, action authority, and attributed absence machine-readable while keeping apparatus, covenant, and claims separate.

akouo_version.description: Optional AKOÚŌ contract version that produced this output (e.g. "0.6"), so stores and benchmarks can pin records to a contract.

apparatus.description: Optional v0.6 declaration of the listening apparatus. Reduced or machine listening that hides its own technical conditions repeats the phantasmagoria it should expose; declare the substrate instead.

listener.description: Optional v0.6 declaration of who or what listened: human, agent, or hybrid, and under which process.

memory.description: Optional v0.6 link block connecting this output to stored sound-memories (earworm-style akousma records): the record this listening belongs to, the records it consulted, and a lineage note.

covenant.description: Optional v0.7 declaration of the listening covenant this output was produced under: the covenant's identity plus honest absence — what was withheld under its rules, counted and attributed, never described.

listening_context.description: Optional v0.8 accountable listening context. Current producers SHOULD emit it; omission remains valid for v0.7 and older records.

substrate.description: The listening architecture family. Each substrate carries its own epistemic stance: an ASR cascade reduces listening to transcription, a codec-token model to token prediction, a DSP toolchain to measurement.

perception_sources.description: Named perception streams that fed this output (e.g. 'moss-audio caption pass', 'browser FFT features', 'human field notes').

model_ids.description: Model identifiers involved, when models were used.

sample_rate_hz.description: Sample rate the listening substrate actually received, when known.

channels.description: Channel count the listening substrate actually received, when known.

bandwidth_limit_hz.description: Upper frequency bound the substrate can register, when known. Claims above this bound belong in undetermined.

known_blind_spots.description: What this apparatus structurally cannot hear or verify (e.g. 'mono input: no stereo image claims', 'no absolute playback level').

capture_notes.description: Known facts about the capture chain: microphone, recorder, room, codec history.

akousma_id.description: Identifier of the stored sound-memory record this listening belongs to or updates, when a store is in use.

akousmata_refs.description: Identifiers of stored records consulted or compared during this listening.

lineage_note.description: Short note on how this sound relates to its lineage (derived from, variant of, response to, recurrence of).

id.description: The covenant's stable slug, optionally versioned: 'river-covenant/2'.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the covenant's source text: identity without content.

rule.description: The covenant rule (verb or original line) under which material was withheld.

subject.description: What was withheld, named at category level only (transcript, speech, location, …) — never described in content.

withheld.description: Honest absence: what this hearing declined to analyze, reveal, or retain, attributed to the rule that asked for it. Withheld material is counted and categorized, never characterized.

commitments.description: How many non-executable commitments the covenant carries; their text stays with the covenant, not with every output.
